title: allium arvense subsp. aestivum
intro: Allium arvense subsp. aestivum, also known as wild garlic or bear's garlic, is a plant species that belongs to the onion family (Alliaceae) and is characterized by its strong garlic-like odor and flavor. The medicinal properties of Allium arvense subsp. aestivum include its antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal properties, which make it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of consuming wild garlic include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and support respiratory health. Herbal preparations of Allium arvense subsp. aestivum can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
